name: SendPulse API
what_it_does: SendPulse REST API — omnichannel marketing automation platform supporting email, SMS, web push, Viber, WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and chatbots via a unified API, enabling agents to orchestrate multi-channel marketing campaigns and automation flows.
use_cases: Agents triggering omnichannel marketing campaigns (email + SMS + push) via single SendPulse API for coordinated outreach
Chatbot automation — agents creating and managing SendPulse chatbot flows for WhatsApp and Messenger without code
Email list management — agents syncing subscriber lists, managing segments, and triggering email campaigns based on user actions
Transactional messaging — agents sending transactional emails, SMS, and push notifications for order confirmations and alerts
Automation workflows — agents triggering SendPulse automation sequences based on CRM events and customer lifecycle stages
not_for: Developer-first email — SendPulse is a marketing platform, not a transactional email API; use SendGrid or Postmark for pure transactional email
Enterprise marketing automation — Marketo or HubSpot have more sophisticated enterprise automation features
US-only teams — SendPulse is popular in Eastern Europe and Latin America; support and documentation best in those regions
best_when: You need a cost-effective omnichannel marketing platform (email + SMS + push + chatbots) with a single API, especially for non-US markets where pricing is competitive.
avoid_when: You need sophisticated enterprise marketing automation or developer-first transactional email — SendPulse is best as a marketer-managed platform with API for automation.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for machine-to-machine access. Client ID and Client Secret from SendPulse account. Access token expires after 1 hour and must be refreshed. No scope granularity — full account access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Email: from $8/month (based on subscribers); SMS: pay-per-message; Push: from $8/month; chatbots included on paid plans
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for small lists. Pricing competitive for Eastern European and Latin American markets. SMS pricing varies by country. Chatbot and push notifications available on all paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: 60 req/min for most endpoints; bulk send limits vary by account tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with reasonable documentation. OAuth token management adds complexity. Error responses include error codes. API documentation available in English but quality is uneven across endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Campaign sends are not idempotent — duplicate API calls send duplicate campaigns. Subscriber add/update operations are idempotent by email address. Agents must track campaign send state externally.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth access token expires every 1 hour — agents must implement token refresh logic and handle 401 responses by re-authenticating
Campaign sends are not idempotent — agents must track whether a campaign was sent to avoid duplicate sends
SMS pricing and availability vary significantly by country — verify coverage before building SMS workflows
API documentation quality is uneven — some endpoints are poorly documented; test before relying on undocumented behavior
Domain verification required for email sending — agents must ensure sending domain is verified before triggering campaigns

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. OAuth2 token-based auth is better than API key for some scenarios. EU and US data centers. No SOC2. CAN-SPAM compliant for US email marketing.
